acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] pending_events is bounded by size/bytes or removed entirely.
- [ ] Overflow behavior is explicit (drop policy + metric/log).
- [ ] Tests or assertions cover the cap behavior.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/manager.rs, src/daemon/repl/server.rs
description:
**Problem**
Both inbound and outbound replication loops buffer events before streaming is established, but the pending_events Vec has no size/byte bound. If handshake stalls or a peer stays non-streaming, this can grow without bound and consume memory.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/repl/manager.rs: pending_events: Vec<BroadcastEvent> grows while !streaming.
- src/daemon/repl/server.rs: pending_events: Vec<BroadcastEvent> grows while !streaming.

**Why this hurts**
A slow or stuck peer can cause unbounded memory growth in the daemon, which is brittle and hard to reason about.
design:
**Design**
Option A: Replace pending_events with a bounded VecDeque that caps both event count and bytes (use limits.max_event_batch_events / max_event_batch_bytes or broadcaster hot-cache limits). Drop oldest events and record a metric when overflowing.
Option B: Remove pending_events entirely and rely on hot_cache + WANT after handshake, avoiding buffering while not streaming.

Either way, prevent unbounded growth and keep behavior deterministic.
title: Replication pending_events queue is unbounded

description:
## What's Wrong
flake.nix has doCheck=false to avoid test failures during nix build. This means `nix build` won't catch test regressions.

## Where
- flake.nix:42-43

## Why It Matters
Users installing via nix won't get test validation. If tests break, nix users get broken builds.

## Options
1. Fix whatever causes tests to fail in nix sandbox (likely git/network related)
2. Add a separate `nix flake check` that runs tests outside the build
3. Accept the gap and rely on CI (current state)
title: Nix flake skips tests (doCheck=false) - CI gap

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rich realistic fixtures assert alias-shaped deps and other scenario-specific semantics after migration.
- [ ] Local/remote divergence coverage proves same-edge joins do not create duplicate logical deps or inflated dots.
- [ ] Detect tests pin exact truth-table values, not just field presence.
- [ ] Each checked-in default fixture runs `detect -> dry-run -> migrate -> strict verify`.
- [ ] Remote/pushed-path coverage is strong enough for the documented operator workflow.
description:
**Problem**
The new realistic fixture corpus improved migration coverage, but the adversarial review found several unproven semantics:
- alias-shaped legacy deps in `rich_workflow` are present in the corpus but never asserted after migration
- the local/remote divergence pair does not prove same-edge joins avoid dot inflation or other duplicate-edge regressions
- detect coverage mostly checks field presence instead of the exact truth-table values from the design doc
- not every checked-in fixture runs the full `detect -> dry-run -> migrate -> strict verify` flow

That means the suite can still miss public contract regressions even though the corpus looks realistic on disk.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/*, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Turn the fixture corpus into a real regression gate instead of a mostly smoke-style suite.

1. Add explicit assertions for alias-shaped legacy deps, deleted deps, and other scenario-specific semantics carried by the checked-in fixtures.
2. Add a deterministic join assertion for the shared local/remote edge case so migration proves it does not inflate dots or duplicate logical deps.
3. Tighten detect assertions to the exact truth-table contract values (`deps_format`, `notes_present`, `checksums_present`, `effective_format_version`, `reasons`).
4. Ensure every default fixture runs through `detect`, `migrate to 1 --dry-run`, `migrate to 1`, and strict post-migration verification.
5. Add or tighten pushed/remote-path coverage where the docs or public contract depend on it.
title: Migration follow-up: harden realistic fixture coverage against contract regressions

description:
**Problem**
The latest adversarial review found that `bd migrate detect` and `bd migrate to 1 --dry-run` can silently fall back to `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` when live remote preview fails, even though that tracking ref may be stale. Today both paths call `open_remote_store_preview(...).ok().flatten()` and then keep going, which means transport/auth/tempdir failures or a deleted/moved remote store ref can produce stale classification instead of either a live preview or a safer local-only result.

That drifts from the hard-cutover requirement that detect and dry-run classify the same real source set they can safely observe.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs

**Design**
Make live preview failure explicit instead of silently trusting stale tracking refs.

1. Distinguish successful live preview, no live remote store ref, and live preview failure.
2. Do not classify against stale tracking refs when live preview failed.
3. Preserve the existing local fallback behavior when local state exists and remote preview is unavailable, but keep the remote side out of the classification in that case.
4. If there is no trustworthy local state to fall back to, surface the preview failure instead of pretending a stale tracking ref is current.
5. Add regression tests with broken origin + stale tracking refs for both detect and dry-run.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Detect does not use stale tracking refs after live remote preview failure.
- [ ] Dry-run does not use stale tracking refs after live remote preview failure.
- [ ] Local fallback still works when origin is broken but a local store ref exists.
- [ ] Tests cover broken-origin + stale-tracking cases for detect and dry-run.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 4cd04383: detect and dry-run no longer trust stale tracking refs when live remote preview fails. They now use live remote preview when available, fall back to local-only classification only when a local store exists, and surface the preview error otherwise. Coverage now includes broken-origin stale-tracking detect and dry-run regressions. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: detect and dry-run must not classify against stale tracking when live remote preview is unavailable

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All match arms updated to ctx/payload.
- [ ] RequestContext extraction tests updated and green.
description:
**Problem**
Daemon request routing (`coord.rs`, `server.rs`) matches on the old Request shape. After refactor, it must destructure `{ ctx, payload }` and pass repo/meta/read correctly.

**Design**
- Update `daemon/coord.rs` `handle_request` match arms to `{ ctx, payload }` and pass:
  - `ctx.repo.path` for repo
  - `ctx.meta` or `ctx.read` for meta/read
  - payload struct directly to mutation/query handlers
- Update `daemon/server.rs` RequestContext extraction, read gate request building, and any request‑type detection to use ctx/payload.
- Keep RequestContext fields identical (repo, namespace, actor_id, client_request_id, read_consistency tags).
- Update `daemon/server.rs` tests that construct Request variants.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All daemon request routing compiles with ctx/payload Request.
- [ ] RequestContext tests still validate the same extracted fields.
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-rs daemon::server` passes.

**Files:**
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/coord.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/server.rs
design: Follow Phase 4 in REFACTORING_PLAN.md. Focus on mechanical destructuring and keep behavior stable.
title: Daemon routing: update coord/server to ctx+payload

description:
**Problem**
`tests/critical_path.rs` and `tests/migration.rs` share a single runtime/data dir for the entire test process via `test_runtime_dir()` (OnceLock + `/tmp/beads-test-runtime-<pid>`). That means all tests in both modules share the same XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/BD_DATA_DIR and the same daemon socket. There is no cleanup of that directory, and parallel test runs can cross-talk through the shared daemon/state. This undermines realtime phase test isolation and makes failures order-dependent.

Evidence:
- `tests/critical_path.rs` lines 14-28 define `test_runtime_dir()` with OnceLock and a fixed path.
- `tests/migration.rs` lines 11-25 define the same pattern and reuse it for BD env.

**Design**
- Replace `test_runtime_dir()` with a per-test TempDir runtime (owned by the test fixture / TestRepo).
- Make `TestRepo` carry `runtime_dir: TempDir` and `data_dir: PathBuf`, and update `bd()` to use those paths.
- Reuse the shared shutdown helper from the daemon teardown bead so each runtime is cleaned up on Drop.
- Avoid shared globals so tests can run in parallel without state collisions.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `tests/critical_path.rs` and `tests/migration.rs` no longer use OnceLock-based runtime dirs.
- [ ] Each test uses a unique TempDir for XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/BD_DATA_DIR and it is removed on Drop.
- [ ] Tests can run with `--test-threads=N` without sharing a daemon/socket.

**Files:**
- tests/critical_path.rs
- tests/migration.rs
- tests/fixtures/daemon_runtime.rs (new, shared helper)
title: tests: critical_path/migration share runtime dir

description: Store bootstrap currently persists metadata and initializes WAL index through separate durable operations. Introduce a single bootstrap state transition type so partial initialization cannot leave divergent durable truth across metadata and index stores.
title: P0: Make store bootstrap meta+index initialization one atomic durable state

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] canonicalize_path resolves symlinks
- [ ] canonicalize_path calls realpath on macOS
- [ ] normalize_path_for_comparison lowercases on macOS/Windows
- [ ] paths_equal handles case-insensitive FS correctly
- [ ] Fallback works if realpath unavailable
- [ ] Works on macOS with mixed-case input
- [ ] Works on Linux (no-op)
- [ ] Works on Windows (uses std::fs::canonicalize)
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] Tests cover symlinks and case variations
title: Implement macOS case-insensitive path canonicalization using realpath

description:
**Problem**
The canonical nextest-backed fast and slow tiers are green, but plain libtest parallelism inside `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e -- --nocapture` still leaves the tailnet-heavy cases (`repl_daemon_crash_restart_tailnet_roundtrip`, `repl_daemon_pathological_tailnet_roundtrip`) hanging when the whole `repl_e2e` binary runs concurrently in one process.

**Evidence**
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e -- --nocapture` hangs in the two tailnet-heavy tests under default libtest threading.
- The same slice passes with `--test-threads=1`.
- The canonical nextest-backed runners are green after fencing tailnet tests into the `tailnet-fault-injection` group.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Root cause of libtest in-process contention is understood
- [ ] Either the `repl_e2e` binary becomes stable under libtest parallelism or the non-canonical limitation is intentionally encoded and documented
- [ ] Canonical nextest receipts stay green after the fix

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs`
- `.config/nextest.toml`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Root cause and resolution, 2026-03-14: `ReplRig` was losing daemon ownership during bootstrap because `bootstrap_replica()` used `bd init`, which autostarts `bd daemon run` through the short-lived CLI helper and reparents the daemon to PID 1. I fixed bootstrap and restart ownership by spawning `bd daemon run` explicitly from the fixture, keeping `Child` handles, and driving `Init`/status over `IpcClient::for_runtime_dir(...).with_autostart(false)` via shared `fixtures::bd_runtime` helpers. Residual libtest-only contention remained between `repl_daemon_crash_restart_tailnet_roundtrip` and `repl_daemon_pathological_tailnet_roundtrip`; the suite is now stable under plain `cargo test ... daemon::repl_e2e` by encoding the same isolation boundary nextest already uses, with a shared single-flight guard on those two external daemon+proxy fault-injection tests. Receipts: `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e -- --nocapture` -> 12/12 passed in 17.06s (artifact `tmp/perf/tests-20260314-164034-libtest-repl-e2e-serialized-fault-tests`); targeted `cargo nextest run --profile fast -p beads-rs --test integration --all-features daemon::repl_e2e:: --status-level none --final-status-level slow` passed on the ownership fix path (artifact `tmp/perf/tests-20260314-162851-repl-e2e-bootstrap-fix-nextest`). AGENTS.md now records the explicit daemon-ownership and libtest single-flight rules.

title: libtest-parallel daemon::repl_e2e tailnet cases still contend under cargo test
